title: Xtra Cheesy Mexican Epoisses Quesadilla
description: A nostalgic Oaxacan dish featuring epoisses and ferment jang, this recipe brings a citrusy twist to the classic Mexican quesadilla, with a focus on cheese pull and fodmap friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Preheat the ceramic tagine over medium heat, adding the olive oil to coat the bottom.
In a bowl, mix together the softened epoisses cheese, ferment jang, and lime juice until well combined.
Add the chopped cilantro, salt, and black pepper to the cheese mixture and stir until the cilantro is evenly distributed.
Place a large corn tortilla in the tagine and sprinkle a quarter of the cheese mixture onto half of the tortilla.
Sprinkle a quarter of the shredded Oaxacan cheese over the epoisses mixture.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the cheese, and cook for 2-3 minutes or until the tortilla is lightly browned and the cheese is melted.
Flip the quesadilla and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the other side is also lightly browned.
Repeat the process with the remaining tortillas and cheese mixture.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
